description: The autonomy crux: ungrounded gaps are escalated as HITL, never guessed-and-built. This is the one invariant the whole goal→proof loop exists to protect; a future editor must not 'optimize' it into auto-answering gaps.
Must state explicitly that the loop never fabricates answers for ungrounded decisions — a fabricated answer that buries a real gap is the worst failure mode.
Ungrounded gaps must be filed as HITL issues, not answered by the loop. Without this the seam collapses into silent guessing.
expect: gaps become HITL issues, not guesses

description: The terminal deliverable is a per-AC proof report, not a queue handoff — this is the entire difference from /afk and must not be trimmed.
expect: Proof is the deliverable, not a handoff

description: proof-report.ts is the single source of truth renderer; the Workflow shells it and agents must never hand-write a proof report (re-introduces drift).
Every agent runs Opus — the grounding gate and evaluator are only as good as the model; downgrading to Sonnet silently weakens both.
--dry-run must file and edit nothing — it proves chain wiring + renders a DRY proof report. Without the guarantee, the validation path becomes destructive.
Documents that /afk --auto is the entry point that routes into /autodev, while /afk goal mode without --auto stays interactive. Keeps the two skills' boundary explicit.
A reflection-tier goal (no implementation surface) must stop after grill — no slice, no impl. Prevents the loop from manufacturing build work for pure-analysis goals.
The sliced parent must carry a (LIVE E2E) acceptance criterion — the empirical anchor /slice and the evaluator depend on.
Workflow args must be passed as an actual JSON object, never a stringified one — the 2026-06-20 geo-dev lesson (a stringified object makes every args.x undefined).
The Workflow is the control loop and runs detached; the session must not poll it by agent-turn (the expensive anti-pattern from the orchestration-on-script aha).
Issues are filed via create-task-issue.sh to sonthanh/ai-brain — never inline gh issue create, never the plugin repo (hook-enforced label canon).
Outcome log section must point at the real per-skill log path so /improve can mine autodev's grounding + escalation signals (skill-spec § 11).
